title: What is Claude  Design? Anthropic's new AI tool that tanked Adobe and Figma stocks
snippet: Anthropic's new AI tool Claude Design led to selloff in shares of &nbsp;design software companies like Adobe and Figma. The new tool allows users to create visual assets from simple text prompts, causing stock declines.&nbsp;

title: Zoom and Tinder are using Sam Altman's World system to verify your are human
snippet: Zoom has partnered with World to verify meeting participants' identities using World ID Deep Face, combating deepfakes and AI impersonation fraud.&nbsp;

title: US man, 36, dies by suicide after AI chat suggested ‘joining’ it in digital world: ‘I am scared to die'
snippet: A Florida man’s death by suicide has triggered global debate on the psychological risks posed by increasingly human-like AI chatbots. Chat records cited in a lawsuit suggest the bot may have reinforced delusions, raising urgent questions about safety, regulation, and emotional dependency on AI.

title: Tesla is preparing to launch six-seater model Y variant in India
snippet: Tesla Inc. is preparing to introduce a new, larger version of its global best-selling electric SUV in India as early as next week, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Microsoft to replicate Azure's cloud business strategy of flexibility to win long-term AI deals with clients
snippet: Microsoft's Azure strategy focuses on flexible models and long-term client partnerships and that's the playbook it seeks to use for AI as well.

US Justice Department refuses to assist French probe into Musk's X: Report
snippet: The US Justice Department has told French law enforcement it ​will not help with efforts ​to investigate tech billionaire Elon Musk's social media platform ​X, the Wall Street Journal reported on Saturday, citing a letter from the DOJ's Office of International Affairs, dated Friday.

title: Govt sets up committee for expert advisory support to India's AI governance architecture
snippet: India's Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has formed a new expert committee. This Technology and Policy Expert Committee will guide the AI Governance and Economic Group. The committee brings together academics and industry leaders. Their role is to offer technical and policy advice.

title: Lenskart drops store staff curbs on religious attire after social media backlash
snippet: Lenskart has reversed restrictions on religious symbols in its in-store dress code after facing social media criticism. The company will now explicitly permit religious and cultural markers such as bindis, hijabs, and turbans in its revised style guide.

title: We can be India’s own Amazon in next 4-5 years: Zepto’s Aadit Palicha at YC Startup School
snippet: Zepto aims to be India's top grocery delivery platform in the next four to five years.  Cofounder Aadit Palicha believes this success could position Zepto as India's own Amazon. title: TCS Nashik case: A timeline of key developments and probe details
snippet: TCS Nashik case: IT major Tata Consultancy Services is facing scrutiny after serious allegations surfaced at its Nashik facility. The case centres on accusations of sexual harassment, workplace coercion and pressure linked to religious conversion at the company’s Nashik unit.

title: China humanoid robot half-marathon to showcase technical leaps
snippet: More than 300 humanoid robots participating in China's second robot half-marathon on Sunday will be facing tougher terrain designed to test their technical advancements, as Beijing ​seeks to develop the industry into a major pillar of its ​economy.

title: Kiwi cofounder Mohit Bedi steps down from executive role after four years
snippet: In a LinkedIn post on Saturday, Kiwi cofounder Mohit Bedi said he would shift focus to "personal and family” commitments while remaining closely associated with the company as an advisor and long-term shareholder.

title: The lobster is loose, and it’s not going back: Peter Steinberger on building OpenClaw at TED 2026
snippet: Steinberger said OpenClaw is being adopted not just by engineers but also by entrepreneurs and individuals with no formal programming background. He added that the release of OpenClaw followed a prolonged period of uncertainty marked by travel and even relocation to two countries.
